10.reason: [The comparison function should cast to struct word*, not char*. This causes incorrect sorting because it compares raw memory as if it were strings starting at the beginning of each struct, but the actual string is at offset 0 within the struct. However, more critically, the qsort call passes library[0].ch which is wrong.]

30.reason: [This writes beyond the last valid character. If the loop exits because word_tem[i] is non-letter, then the valid letters are from 0 to i-1, so null terminator should be at word_tem[i], not i+1. This may cause buffer overflow or undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This writes beyond the last valid character. If the loop exits because word_tem[i] is non-letter, then the valid letters are from 0 to i-1, so null terminator should be at word_tem[i], not i+1. This may cause buffer overflow or undefined behavior.]

49.reason: [Incorrect arguments to qsort: first argument should be library (the array), not library[0].ch (a char array inside the first element). Also, the size should be sizeof(struct word), and the comparison function must compare struct word elements properly.]
